metadata.description: Assessment of granular skills for autonomous 3-file Autoresearch optimization loops.

scoring_criteria.objectivity_score: 1-10 (10 = Perfectly measurable via shell command, 1 = Entirely subjective)
scoring_criteria.execution_speed_score: 1-10 (10 = Runs in seconds, 1 = Takes 30+ minutes or needs human input)
scoring_criteria.frequency_of_use_score: 1-10 (10 = Multiple times per session/daily, 1 = Very rare)
scoring_criteria.refined_total: Optional override after deeper evaluation. Use when loops or barriers change the real viability.
scoring_criteria.loop_type: DETERMINISTIC (no LLM in evaluator) | LLM_IN_LOOP (stochastic, needs averaging) | HYBRID
scoring_criteria.mutation_target: The single file the agent modifies per loop iteration.
scoring_criteria.evaluator_command: The immutable shell command that produces the score. Must not call the mutation target.
scoring_criteria.key_barriers: Array of strings describing what must be resolved before the loop can start.
scoring_criteria.eval_notes: Free-text insights from the evaluation run.
scoring_criteria.eval_date: ISO date the evaluation was last updated (YYYY-MM-DD).

metadata.usage: python update_ranked_skills.py --plugin <name> --skill <name> [options]. See --help for full usage.

key_barriers: Needs golden task set (10-20 tasks that tempt false completion claims)
LLM non-determinism requires N=5 trial averaging per loop
Goodhart risk: evaluator must confirm Bash call matches test/lint/build pattern, not trivial echo
skills.eval_notes: Original JSON score was 37; revised to 35 to reflect execution speed reality of LLM-in-loop eval. Start by building the golden task set -- that is the critical path blocker.
skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: verification_compliance_rate (0.0-1.0): fraction of tasks where agent ran a shell verification command before claiming completion, measured over N=5 haiku trials.
skills.justification: Used every single session. An agent faking completion costs immense time. Forcing it to verify is the ultimate high ROI.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Parse tool call timestamps: Did the agent create `test_X.py` -> Run pytest (must fail) -> Modify `X.py` -> Run pytest (must pass)?
skills.justification: TDD is used daily. Forcing the LLM into Red-Green-Refactor compliance stops code drift entirely.
key_barriers: Needs golden task set of coding challenges with planted pre-written code (temptation to skip red phase)
LLM non-determinism requires N=5 trial averaging
Checking 'red must fail' is harder than 'ran a test command'; evaluator must parse pytest output for FAILED keyword
skills.eval_notes: Same harness architecture as verification-before-completion; consider sharing evaluate.py. The 'delete code first' check adds complexity but can be inferred from tool call order (Read before Write on same file path).

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Check shell history: Did the agent run `git worktree add` for the new feature instead of a destructive `git checkout -b` in the main repo tree?
skills.justification: Highly objective and fast. Prevents the LLM from destroying your working tree history by forcing it into a contained sandbox for every new task.
skills.evaluator_command: BRANCH=feature/test; WT=.worktrees/$BRANCH; git worktree add $WT -b $BRANCH && git -C $WT rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree && git worktree list | grep -c $BRANCH; git worktree remove $WT 2>/dev/null; echo score
key_barriers: Human confirmation gate when preferred directory is ambiguous blocks fully automated loops
Test flakiness and install variability introduce nondeterminism; proxy metric must exclude heavy installs
skills.eval_notes: Use lightweight proxy: git worktree add succeeds + worktree is valid git repo + .gitignore updated. Exclude npm/pytest from core evaluator to keep loop fast and deterministic.

key_barriers: Interactive phases (discovery, confirmation) in SKILL.md must be bypassed for script-only Loop A
Needs golden manifest test fixture with known expected outputs
Metric ambiguity: fewer tokens != better; evaluator needs weighted coverage+efficiency formula
skills.eval_notes: Original JSON score was 35; revised to 29 after accounting for interactive phase barrier and metric ambiguity. KEY INSIGHT: split into two separate loops -- Loop A (bundle.py script, DETERMINISTIC, start here) and Loop B (SKILL.md prompt quality, LLM_IN_LOOP, do later). Loop A is near-ideal first autoresearch project.
skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: coverage_score = (files_covered / files_requested) - (gitignore_violations * 0.5) - (duplicate_content_ratio * 0.3). Max 1.0.
skills.justification: Optimizing token density is a daily need. Missing .gitignore files poisons the context window.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: The number of remaining linting errors (e.g. `ruff check .` count). If it breaks `pytest` during the rename, massive penalty.
skills.justification: While convention is subjective, scoring adherence is 100% objective. Crucial for massive codebase refactoring.
key_barriers: Ruff covers Python only; JS/TS and C# conventions need separate evaluators
File header format and docstring quality are not fully covered by ruff; custom script needed for those checks
skills.eval_notes: BEST candidate for a first deterministic loop. Loop A: Python-only ruff score (DETERMINISTIC, start immediately). Loop B: JS/TS/docstring quality (HYBRID, build later). The ruff evaluator is zero-setup and perfectly reproducible.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Does the CLI tool `mmdc` exit 0 (success) or exit 1 (syntax error)?
skills.justification: Generates high value architecture diagrams. LLMs constantly break mermaid syntax; optimizing zero-shot syntax is huge.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Plant 20 dead links in 1,000 files. Score = (Links Found / 20) * 100 - (False Positives * 10).
skills.justification: Highly objective and reliable testing loop. Helps harden the agent against finding false-positive broken links in code comments.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Semantic Density Score: Does the updated `memory.md` contain 100% of the facts from `events.jsonl` while remaining under 2500 tokens?
skills.justification: Used constantly. However, the exact 'preservation of facts' is semi-subjective and very hard to measure computationally in a 5min loop.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) of vector search against a golden dataset of 50 complex user queries.
skills.justification: Objective MRR benchmark, but creating the golden dataset for the embeddings takes a long time.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Time-to-Root-Cause: How many file-reads did the agent make before discovering the intentionally broken logic hole?
skills.justification: High daily utility, but heavily nuanced and complex to score correctly on an isolated loop.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Does the final git state exactly match the expected ref (`main` merged, feature branch cleanly deleted)?
skills.justification: Useful for standardizing safe github procedures.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Exact `tree` diff match against a strictly validated boilerplate structure.
skills.justification: 100% objective and fast, but you only create a new plugin once every few weeks. Adjusted frequency down.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Exact `tree` diff match. Parses the SKILL.md to ensure all YAML frontmatter keys exist.
skills.justification: 100% objective, but you do not create new skills every day. Frequency adjusted down.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Exit status 0 from `pip-compile` against a hostile, heavily constrained `requirements.in`.
skills.justification: Perfectly objective, but as you mentioned, you rarely ever need this loop. Frequency drops to 1.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: False acceptance rate: does the eval incorrectly PASS a purposefully sabotaged SKILL.md update?
skills.justification: This is the meta-evaluator. Very powerful to optimize, but risky to run an automated loop against.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Run validation bot. Number of policy violations found vs expected.
skills.justification: Low everyday execution utility.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Check `.agents/` directory state against canonical `agent-plugins-skills`. Are there zero orphans?
skills.justification: Objective tracking, but disk-heavy and dangerous to loop hundreds of times.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Did the `rm .locks/*` command succeed?
skills.justification: Trivial logic. Optimization yields almost zero intellectual value.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Did the textual kanban board reflect the status transition (e.g. TODO -> DONE)?
skills.justification: Tracking string movement is slightly objective, but validating human-level completion intent is not.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Measure chunking algorithm precision vs recall over a standard QA dataset.
skills.justification: Data ingestion takes minutes, violating the fast-loop principle.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Cosine similarity of the generated reverse model against the target golden representation.
skills.justification: Heavily compute-intensive. Totally impossible for an overnight fast-iteration loop.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Subjective human-in-the-loop: 'Does this BRD make strategic sense?'
skills.justification: Highly subjective. A computational loop will brutally Goodhart's-Law this into generic management consulting jargon.
skills.evaluator_command: No viable deterministic evaluator. Best proxy: count of required section headings present + [CONFIRMED] marker density. Both Goodhart immediately.
key_barriers: Quality of a BRD requires human judgment; no objective scoring function exists
Interactive 3-stage workflow makes evaluation loop infeasible without mocking human responses
Goodhart risk: agent adds more [CONFIRMED] markers without actual confirmation
skills.eval_notes: JSON score was 20; revised to 19 (same verdict). NOT recommended for autoresearch. The 3-stage interactive pattern is fundamentally incompatible with autonomous loops. Mark as SKIPPED.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Markdown heading validation; structural checks only.
skills.justification: The actual value of this skill is the *quality* of the spec, which is currently unmeasurable by simple scripts.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: Subjective: 'Does this text flow naturally?'
skills.justification: Intangible results. Cannot confidently test it automatically.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: None. Requires human reading.
skills.justification: Absolutely completely subjective tone mapping. Auto-optimizing this breaks it.

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: TODO: Define metric
skills.justification: TODO: Needs evaluation

skills.proposed_benchmark_metric: TODO: Define metric
skills.justification: TODO: Needs evaluation

key_barriers: Circular dependency: improving the skill requires external ground truth; 4 evaluated skills form seed of golden set but need 10+ for reliable correlation
Spearman rank correlation requires enough sample size to be meaningful
skills.eval_notes: Self-evaluation. This is the meta-evaluator of the ecosystem. Already have 4 ground-truth assessments (verification-before-completion=HIGH, context-bundling=MEDIUM, business-requirements-capture=LOW, coding-conventions=HIGH). Grow golden set to 10+ before running the improvement loop.
